project_type: React + TypeScript SPA with Vite bundler, AWS backend (SAM/Lambda/AgentCore)
build_system: Vite for frontend, SAM for backend. Frontend build: tsc && vite build
test_framework: No test framework configured in frontend package.json. Backend has a test_websocket.py for the agent.
verification_instructions: 1. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in frontend/ to verify TypeScript compiles. 2. Run `npm run build` in frontend/ to verify Vite build succeeds.
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network mode - npm install works. Node 22 available. No Docker needed for frontend validation.
contribution_requirements: No CONTRIBUTING.md found. Follow existing code patterns (contexts pattern, service singletons).
key_patterns: Contexts pattern: AuthContext.tsx provides auth state via useAuth() hook. Services are singleton instances exported from modules (authService, apiService, websocketService). WebSocketService class uses event emitter pattern with on/off/emit. Chat.tsx currently owns entire WS lifecycle including connect, reconnect on visibility/network, disconnect on unmount. The connect() method requires sessionId but the sessionId is actually sent per-message via sendQuery(). The backend websocket-connect.js Lambda makes sessionId optional in the presigned URL generation (it uses a fallback UUID if none provided).
directory_structure: frontend/src/contexts/ - React context providers (AuthContext). frontend/src/services/ - Service classes (auth, api, websocket, userService). frontend/src/components/ - React components (Chat, Home, Layout, Login, etc.). frontend/src/config/ - API config. backend/functions/ - Lambda functions.
